records.ety: [F. nativit, L. nativitas. See Native, and cf. Navet.]

defs: The coming into life or into the world; birth; also, the circumstances attending birth, as time, place, manner, etc.
quotes: I have served him from the hour of my nativity.
Thou hast left . . . the land of thy nativity.
These in their dark nativity the deep Shall yield us, pregnant with infernal flame.

defs: A picture representing or symbolizing the early infancy of Christ. The simplest form is the babe in a rude cradle, and the heads of an ox and an ass to express the stable in which he was born.
A representation of the positions of the heavenly bodies as the moment of one's birth, supposed to indicate one's future destinies; a horoscope.
